properties.remark: RETIRED NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED ROOF SHINGLES BLOWN OFF HOUSE.

NWS STORM SURVEY ESTIMATED 60 TO 65 MPH WIND GUST DESTROYED ANNOUNCERS BOOTH AT MERIDIAN LIONS CLUB RODEO GROUNDS. IN ADDITION...4 SETS OF UNSECURED GRANDSTAND BLEACHERS WERE FLIPPED UPSIDE DOWN AND ROLLED OVER A FENCE INTO THE MIDDLE OF THE RODEO GROUNDS.
DEPUTY REPORTS BRIEF TOUCHDOWN OF TORNADO 8 MILES NORTHEAST OF JEROME AT 525 PM MDT. EF0 RATING. NO DAMAGE.
TWO PHOTOGRAPHS OF WHAT APPEARS TO BE A LANDSPOUT OFF THE INTERSTATE ABOUT 10 MILES WNW OF MOUNTAIN HOME. FROM THE SIZE AND APPEARANCE WE ARE ESTIMATING IT AS AN EF-0 ON THE ENHANCED FUJITA SCALE.
SPOTTER MEASURED THUNDERSTORM DOWNBURST WIND GUST SPEED TO 57 KNOTS WITH SUSTAINS WINDS OF 38 KNOTS FROM THE NNW. NO DAMAGE NOTED AT THIS TIME. MAIN CORE OF STORM NW OF STATION.

1.5 INCHES OF RAIN IN 20 MINUTES. FIELDS COVERED IN HAIL AND DEBRIS PILED UP SEVERAL FEET DEEP BY RUSHING WATER. NO DAMAGE TO STRUCTURES.

OLD CHAPPELS BUILDING SEVERELY DAMAGED. POWER LINES DOWN.
REPORT FOR FACEBOOK. ROOF RIPPED OFF BARN AND IRRIGATION PIVOTS TURNED OVER. PEA SIZE HAIL AND HEAVY RAIN. TIME IS ESTIMATED.

WATER AND MUD FLOWED ACROSS SECTIONS OF HIGHWAY 78 BETWEEN MURPHY AND EAGLE VIEW SUBDIVISION ABOUT 4 MILES NORTH OF MURPHY. A CAR WAS WASHED OFF A ROAD AND A PERSON WAS RESCUED FROM THE VEHICLE. RABBIT CREEK CAUSED EROSION OF A SECONDARY ROAD NEAR MURPHY.
CWOP E2058 STATION
TWO MUDSLIDE ON THE BANKS AND LOWMEN ROAD JUST BEFORE GARDEN VALLEY TRAPPED TWO VEHICLES.
RECEIVED 0.78 IN ABOUT 20 MINUTES WHICH CAUSE MUDSLIDES
NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED ROAD FLOODING NEAR UPSTICK AND LOCUST GROVE...DEPTH OF 1 FOOT IN PLACES.
MUDSLIDE NORTH OF BANKS ON HIGHWAY 55. TIME IS ESTIMATED FROM RADAR. RELAYED THROUGH LOCAL MEDIA.
RECEIVED AN ADDITIONAL 0.50 INCH OF RAIN IN 10 MINUTES. MUDSLIDES SEEN NEAR RESIDENCE.

SPOTTER REPORTED THUNDERSTORM WITH HEAVY RAIN. RAINFALL FROM THUNDERSTORM RANGING FROM 0.75 TO 1.50 INCHES TOTAL STORM ACCUMULATION. SPOTTER REPORTED FLASH FLOODING NEAR MILE MARKER 54 DURING TRAVEL THROUGH GRANDVIEW...HEIGHT OF WATER ON NEARBY ROAD UP TO 1/2 FOOT...ALSO REPORTED SEVERAL FARM HOMES NEAR GRANDVIEW HAD BEEN EXPERIENCING SOME FLOODING AS FIELDS UNABLE TO ABSORB THE BRUNT OF THE ABRUPT HEAVY RAIN.
MUD SLIDE REPORTED AT MILE POST 114 BETWEEN PRAIRE ROAD AND ANDERSON DAM ROAD ON U.S. HIGHWAY 20
WATER RUNNING IN CASTLE CREEK AT OREANA FOR THE FIRST TIME THIS YEAR. WATER ALSO RUNNING IN THE DRIVEWAY APPARENTLY FROM OVERFLOWING DITCH.
